questions.question: Explain the difference between checked and unchecked exceptions with examples.
keyPoints: Checked exceptions are verified at compile-time; unchecked are at runtime
IOException, SQLException are checked exceptions
NullPointerException, ArithmeticException are unchecked
Compiler forces handling of checked exceptions

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'finally' block? Give an example scenario where it is essential.
keyPoints: Executes regardless of exception occurrence
Used for resource cleanup (closing files, releasing connections)
Executes even if return statement is in try
Essential for database connections, file streams

questions.question: Explain Exception Propagation in Java with an example.
keyPoints: Exception not caught propagates to caller method
Continues up call stack until caught or reaches JVM
If uncaught at top, program terminates with stack trace
Demonstrate with nested method calls showing exception flow

questions.question: Write a Java program to demonstrate try-catch-finally block.
keyPoints: Include try block with exception-causing code
Catch block to handle specific exception type
Finally block with cleanup code
Show output demonstrating flow

questions.question: What is the difference between 'throw' and 'throws' keywords?
keyPoints: throw explicitly throws an exception object; throws declares exceptions
throw is used in method body; throws in method signature
throw can throw only one exception; throws declares multiple
throw transfers control; throws is for delegation

questions.question: Explain try-with-resources feature in Java with an example.
keyPoints: AutoCloseable interface required for resources
Resources automatically closed at end of try block
No need for explicit finally block for resource cleanup
Example: try(FileInputStream fis = new FileInputStream("file.txt"))

questions.question: How do you create a custom exception in Java? Explain with example.
keyPoints: Create class extending Exception (checked) or RuntimeException (unchecked)
Provide constructors (default, parameterized)
Can add custom fields and methods
Demonstrate usage with throw and catch
